result.meeting_context: This is a Visual Arts Committee meeting for the City of Mountain View covering multiple agenda items including roll call, minutes approval, public communications, upcoming agenda topics, unfinished business updates on Capital Improvement Program public art projects, Center for Performing Arts call for artists results, Transit Center Grade Separation project presentation, guidance on public inquiries, and discussion of stipend thresholds for artist evaluations.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Committee called meeting to order and conducted roll call with Vice Chair Hsu having excused absence
points.source: Jesse Cupp : I'm calling meeting to order item 2, roll call into a roll call

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: November 9th, 2022 meeting minutes were approved unanimously by committee vote
points.source: Jesse Cupp : So that passes

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Staff provided context on biodiversity strategy integration with art placement and landscaping, noting K5 policy was provided to consultant team
points.source: John Lang : We did provide the K5 policy to the consultant team that's doing the work

points.speaker: Susi Merhar
points.text: Committee member requested discussion on artist touchpoint strategies and call for artists process improvements
points.source: Susi Merhar : I actually looked at my notes from last meeting and I think our discussion last time made it clear to me that maybe it would be good if we would

points.speaker: Regina Sakols
points.text: Committee member requested original call for artists documentation be included in future packages for better evaluation
points.source: Regina Sakols : I could not find, and I don't know if anybody managed to find the the original call

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Committee discussed concerns about CAFE platform difficulty for artists and potential alternative platforms like slide room
points.source: Jesse Cupp : Apparently a lot of artists don't like using the cafe platform

points.speaker: Toot Too Thomson
points.text: Committee member proposed future agenda item for call for young artists program to display student artwork
points.source: Toot Too Thomson : I actually talked to my son's middle school art, art, art teacher

topics.topic: Capital Improvement Program Art Project Updates

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: No substantial changes since last meeting on Reinstorff Park Aquatic Center, Fayette Park, and maintenance building projects
points.source: John Lang : Really, there's no substantial change since your last meeting

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Updated insurance requirements received for Rankstorff Park maintenance building artists Fernanda and Haroma, allowing agreements to move forward
points.source: John Lang : The one thing I will call your attention to is we did get some updated insurance requirement insurance documents for the artist for Rankstorff Park maintenance

topics.topic: Center for Performing Arts Artist Selection

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Call for artists received 63 responses from 9-county Bay Area region, with committee scoring resulting in top 11 ranked artists
points.source: John Lang : We had 63 responses

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Top scoring artist Martinez confirmed artwork weight compatibility with CPA hanging systems after staff inquiry
points.source: John Lang : The artist was kind enough to provide me the weights of these pieces

points.speaker: Toot Too Thomson
points.text: Committee member clarified that blank scores were intended as zeros, not null values in evaluation system
points.source: Toot Too Thomson : Because I only scored like maybe 11 or 12 artists I left majority of them blank

points.speaker: Regina Sakols
points.text: Committee voted unanimously to select 8 artists instead of 6 for CPA displays with 3 alternates, shortening exhibition periods to accommodate more artists
points.source: Regina Sakols : I do a motion to have eight artists show in the next performance CPA Season of Visual Arts instead of 6

topics.topic: Transit Center Grade Separation Project Presentation

points.speaker: Joy Houghton
points.text: Project currently in final design phase scheduled for completion July 2024, with construction beginning July 2024 through December 2026
points.source: Joy Houghton : The project is currently in final designed phase, which is scheduled to be completed in July of 2024

points.speaker: Joy Houghton
points.text: Project includes vehicle ramp from West Evelyn to Shoreline, shared use path, undercrossings for pedestrians and bikes, and new pickup/drop-off areas
points.source: Joy Houghton : This image here shows the major elements of the project

points.speaker: Joy Houghton
points.text: Multiple opportunities identified for public art integration including undercrossings, concourse area, surface lighting, landscaping, and architectural elements
points.source: Joy Houghton : There are many opportunities for that in the project

points.speaker: Robert Gonzales
points.text: Policy K5 applies with project budget over $90 million, capping visual arts component at $400,000 for art and potential artist stipend models
points.source: Robert Gonzales : So Policy K5 comes into play here

points.speaker: Joy Houghton
points.text: Staff will return to VAC in early 2023 with public art location recommendations and initiate call for artists process
points.source: Joy Houghton : Staff will continue to coordinate with the project design team and finalize public art location recommendations and return to the Visual Art Committee early next year

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Committee members should forward public inquiries about VAC matters to staff for initial response and coordination with clerk's office
points.source: John Lang : So if you do receive inquiries from members of the public you know what's the city's position on this or what does Mountain View do on this related to art if you would be so kind as to forward that to me

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Historical analysis shows VAC provided stipends for three projects: Fire Station #5 ($67,000 budget, $500 stipend), Community Center ($140,000 budget, $5000 stipend), and Rankstorff Aquatic Center ($180,000 budget, $1000 stipend)
points.source: John Lang : There were three projects that actually the VAC committees at those times decided to go through a stipend approach

points.speaker: Susi Merhar
points.text: Committee member expressed preference for holistic approach to artist engagement rather than deciding stipend threshold in isolation
points.source: Susi Merhar : I I feel like I'm a little bit uneasy to decide this personally, kind of in a vacuum

points.speaker: Clifford Bryant
points.text: Committee member advocated for default stipend approach above threshold with option to waive, noting improved quality from artist stipends
points.source: Clifford Bryant : I would go with an idea that we are going to give a stipend in order to finalize the final design

points.speaker: Susi Merhar
points.text: Committee reached consensus on $60,000 threshold triggering automatic discussion about whether to provide artist stipends
points.source: Susi Merhar : It's a trigger for us that we have to have a discussion about a stipend or not

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Staff thanked committee members for thoughtful feedback and contributions throughout the year, extending holiday wishes
points.source: John Lang : I want to thank the VAC committee this year

decisions.text: Committee approved November 9th, 2022 meeting minutes unanimously
Committee voted unanimously to select 8 artists for CPA displays instead of 6, with 3 alternates, shortening exhibition periods
decisions.source: Regina Sakols : I do a motion to have eight artists show in the next performance CPA Season of Visual Arts instead of 6

decisions.text: Staff will coordinate with Public Works team on stipend timing and return with refined threshold policy incorporating $60,000 trigger for stipend discussion
decisions.source: John Lang : So let me go back to the public works folks and just try and understand from a timing frame

decisions.text: Public Works staff will return to VAC in February 2023 with public art location recommendations and initiate call for artists for Transit Center project
decisions.source: Joy Houghton : Staff will continue to coordinate with the project design team and finalize public art location recommendations and return to the Visual Art Committee early next year
